data.title: Integral Ad Science targets 12% growth in 2025 with focus on pre-bid optimization and global expansion
data.description:
Earnings Call Insights: Integral Ad Science Holding Corp. (NASDAQ:IAS) Q4 2024

Management View

  • Revenue for Q4 2024 grew 14% to $153 million, with an adjusted EBITDA margin of 40%. Full-year 2024 revenue reached $530.1 million, marking a 12% increase with a 36% adjusted EBITDA margin. CEO Lisa Utzschneider highlighted, "We significantly exceeded the rule of 40 for the fourth consecutive year since our IPO in 2021."

  • IAS launched new pre-bid optimization tools, including its Meta content block list solution, which reduced wasted ad spend by 71%. The company also expanded its Total Media Quality (TMQ) product to platforms such as Reddit and Pinterest.

  • IAS is advancing into the Chinese market with alpha testing, aiming to capture opportunities in the $140 billion digital ad spend market.

  • Jill Putman, Interim CFO, reported that Q4 revenue exceeded the prior outlook of $148 million to $150 million, while adjusted EBITDA reached $61.4 million, surpassing the guidance range of $55 million to $57 million.

Outlook

  • IAS projects Q1 2025 revenue between $128 million and $131 million, representing 13% year-over-year growth at the midpoint, and adjusted EBITDA of $38 million to $40 million.

  • Full-year 2025 guidance includes revenue in the range of $588 million to $600 million, a 12% year-over-year growth at the midpoint, and adjusted EBITDA of $202 million to $210 million.

  • The company plans to maintain gross margins between 77% and 79% while targeting stronger adoption of its pre-bid optimization and social media products.

Financial Results

  • Adjusted EPS for Q4 2024 was $0.09, and full-year adjusted EBITDA reached $191.3 million.

  • Measurement revenue increased 12% to $59.1 million, with social media channels contributing 25% growth and accounting for 54% of measurement revenue. Publisher revenue rose 30% to $23.4 million, driven by new product adoption and political spending.

  • Gross profit margin for Q4 was 78%, with a full-year margin of 79% at the high end of the company's prior outlook.

  • IAS reduced long-term debt by $30 million during the quarter, leaving $35 million outstanding and ending the quarter with $84 million in cash.

Q&A

  • Mark Kelley, Stifel, asked about the impact of political spending and CTV shifts. CEO Lisa Utzschneider noted limited political headwinds for advertisers, while publishers benefited. Regarding CTV, she stated, "We see real-time bidding as a tailwind for our business as we continue to invest in Publica."

  • Youssef Squali, Truist, inquired about pricing and Meta pre-bid impact. Utzschneider explained, "We were thrilled to see Meta select IAS for the pre-bid optimization. During testing, we achieved a 71% reduction in wasted ad spend."

  • Jason Helfstein, Oppenheimer, asked about the visibility gained through pre-bid tools. Utzschneider emphasized, "We are doubling down on programmatic performance, linking media quality signals with cost data to enhance transparency."

  • Rob Sanderson, Loop Capital, questioned the China expansion strategy. Utzschneider described a "crawl-walk-run" approach, focusing on both "China in" and "China out" strategies for global and domestic brands.

Sentiment Analysis

  • Analysts acknowledged solid quarterly performance but focused on competitive dynamics and product adoption rates. Questions reflected cautious optimism, particularly regarding Oracle integration and new market opportunities.

  • Management maintained a confident tone, particularly when discussing product innovation and strategic initiatives. CEO Utzschneider frequently emphasized IAS’s differentiation and customer value, while addressing challenges like pricing dynamics and adoption timelines.

  • Compared to the previous quarter, the tone of analysts showed slight improvement, reflecting better-than-expected results. Management's confidence also increased, citing successful Oracle onboarding and new market entries.

Quarter-over-Quarter Comparison

  • Q4 2024 revenue growth of 14% outpaced Q3’s 11%. Adjusted EBITDA margin increased from 38% in Q3 to 40%.

  • In contrast to Q3's cautious guidance citing weak CPG and retail spending, Q4 saw stronger advertiser activity in these verticals and continued financial services strength.

  • Management's tone shifted positively as new product launches, such as Meta pre-bid optimization, started to deliver measurable benefits.

Risks and Concerns

  • Analysts raised concerns about pricing pressure from Oracle wins and potential slow adoption of new offerings.

  • Management noted delays in product monetization but emphasized a focus on driving adoption and demonstrating ROI to advertisers.

  • The expansion into China presents challenges, including market complexities and regulatory hurdles.

Final Takeaway

Integral Ad Science Holding Corp. delivered strong Q4 results, driven by double-digit revenue growth across key segments and a robust adjusted EBITDA margin. With a focus on pre-bid optimization, global expansion into markets like China, and enhanced product offerings on platforms such as Meta and TikTok, IAS is well-positioned for 12% revenue growth in 2025. While challenges like pricing dynamics and adoption timelines remain, management's investment in innovative solutions and strategic market entries underscores its confidence in sustaining profitable growth.

Read the full Earnings Call Transcript

data.title: Bath & Body Works gains another bull as Citigroup flips to a Buy
data.description:
After several years of sales and margin declines, Bath & Body Works’ (NYSE:BBWI) sales in the second half of 2024 inflected positively, and FY25 revenue guidance points to growth, Citigroup analyst Paul Lejuez says, all of which suggest a higher multiple will follow.

“Management is demonstrating solid execution, a strong innovation pipeline, and abating home fragrance headwinds, all factors we believe will drive upside to FY25 sales guidance of up 1% to 3%,” said Lejuez.

Despite the negative market reaction to Q1 guidance on Thursday, Lejeuz flipped to a bullish rating for the stock, upgrading Bath & Body Works (NYSE:BBWI) to a Buy from Neutral, and lifting his target price by 20% to $48.

The company acknowledged the challenges it faces from “value-seeking customer behavior and competitive intensity,” but plans to accelerate top-line growth by focusing on product innovation, marketing, and technology, while at the same time boosting its bottom-line through operational efficiencies through cost management.

“Our priorities are to invest for growth and return value to shareholders through dividends and share repurchases,” Bath & Body Works CFO Eva Boratto said on the company’s earnings call.

Towards that end, Lejuez models $820M in free cash flow in 2025 that will allow for excess cash to be distributed to shareholders through buyback and dividends.

The soft guidance “appears prudently conservative,” says Dana Telsey from Telsey Advisory Group, since it assumes a change in U.S. trade policy towards China and the “value seeking customer behavior” CEO Gina Boswell touched on during the earnings call.

“But we continue to see BBWI as an established brand with a loyal customer base within an attractive consumer space characterized by strong purchase frequency of a consumable category,” Telsey adds, reiterating an Outperform rating and $43 price target, implying an ~20% upside to Thursday’s closing price.

Shares of the specialty retailer are back on the offensive, gaining more than 3% and climbing back above its 200-day moving average at $36.21.

data.title: Stocks stay afloat after in-line inflation data, still 'vulnerable' to further selling
data.description:
U.S. stocks on Friday were holding on to gains after a mixed open, as market participants received a key inflation reading that came in-line with expectations.

The three major averages seesawed after a muted start, then ticked upward. Approaching midday, the benchmark S&P 500 (SP500) was last +0.5%, while the tech-heavy Nasdaq Composite (COMP:IND) was also +0.5%. The blue-chip Dow (DJI) was +0.6%.

U.S. Treasury yields were lower. The 10-year yield (US10Y) was down 3 basis points to 4.23%, while the 2-year yield (US2Y) was down 5 basis points to 4.01%.

“Indices still look vulnerable to further selling. Investors are getting increasingly concerned about the imposition of tariffs,” said David Morrison, senior market analyst at Trade Nation.

Before the open, the Core PCE Price Index - widely seen as the Federal Reserve's preferred inflation gauge - came in at +0.3% M/M in January vs. +0.3% expected and +0.2% prior, and +2.6% Y/Y vs. +2.6% consensus and +2.9% (revised from +2.8%).

PCE Price Index, which includes food and energy prices, was +0.3% M/M vs. +0.4% consensus and +0.3% prior, and +2.5% Y/Y vs. +2.5% consensus and +2.6% prior.

"Inflation is still a challenge not just for the Fed but for consumers looking to spend on discretionary items. Earlier this month we learned the CPI surprised to the upside and this morning's data show the Fed's preferred measure of inflation, the PCE deflator, rose 0.3%, which was enough to drive the year-over-year rate lower to 2.5%," Wells Fargo's Tim Quinlan said.

"While risks today look skewed to the upside around inflation, the pricing environment remains highly uncertain. Consumers are price fatigued, and major retailers have acknowledged this. Tariffs are the most obvious threat to the pricing environment and the last few tenths to the Fed's 2% target remain in the crosshair," Quinlan added.

Wall Street caught a bout of heavy selling in the final hour of trading on Thursday, as sentiment took a hit from a post-earnings fall in semiconductor giant Nvidia (NVDA) and more tariff-related moves by U.S. President Donald Trump.

“There was a delayed reaction to Nvidia’s (NVDA) fourth quarter results, which beat expectations, but not by as much as over the last six quarters,” Trade Nation’s Morrison said. “Nvidia’s results, despite upbeat forward guidance, have reinforced concerns over excessive valuations for stocks in ‘Magnificent Seven’,” he added.

data.title: BlackRock, Invesco challenge hedge funds with managed futures ETFs - report
data.description:
Asset managers BlackRock (NYSE:BLK), Invesco (NYSE:IVZ), and Fidelity Investments have launched a wave of exchange-traded funds intended to bring to retail traders strategies once available only to financial elites.

The three firms recently filed to start so-called managed futures ETFs, which use derivatives to ride trends across a range of asset classes, Bloomberg reported on Friday. Their systematic models are designed to adapt to shifting market conditions and offset downturns in traditional portfolios.

While hedge funds known as commodity trading advisers (or CTAs) dominate the trend-chasing strategy, controlling $340B in assets, their growth has stagnated, Bloomberg said, citing BarclayHedge data.

Meanwhile, the ETFs based on the strategy, though tiny by asset size, are growing quickly. Bloomberg data estimates that the ETFs now manage $3.3B, almost double the amount from a year ago. Their growth is helped by lower fees and better performance in 2024, due, in part, to their simpler investment style.

Average return from CTA-style ETFs increased ~7.3% last year, compared with the SG CTA Index, which tracks 20 hedge funds, which gained only 2.4% in the same period, the article said.

Relevant tickers: Simplify Managed Futures ETF (CTA), iMGP DBi Managed Futures Strategy ETF (DBMF), KraneShares Mount Lucas Managed Futures Index Strategy ETF (KMLM), WisdomTree Managed Futures Strategy ETF (WTMF).

data.title: Crypto-linked assets wrapped in red for February as bitcoin, ethereum plunge
data.description:
A number of crypto-linked stocks and exchange-traded funds were headed for losses in February as the selloff in bitcoin (BTC-USD) and ethereum (ETH-USD) put the cryptocurrencies in line for their worst monthly performance since FTX imploded in 2022.

Bitcoin (BTC-USD) entered into February above $102K and has since tumbled. It was facing a monthly loss of ~19%, and earlier Friday risked a drop of ~23% when it fell below $79K. Ethereum (ETH-USD) was looking at a 30% slide for February, with the price down 4% Friday, around $2,221.

Among the factors driving crypto prices down: “President Donald Trump's announcement of impending tariffs … has heightened market uncertainties, leading to a sell-off in risk-sensitive assets, including cryptocurrencies,” Naeem Aslam, chief investment officer at Zaye Capital Markets, said.

Also, investor confidence was “shaken” after hackers stole ~$1.5B in digital assets from crypto exchange Bybit’s ethereum (ETH-USD) cold wallet this month, Aslam said.

Here's how some of crypto-linked ETFs and stocks have moved over the past month, through early Friday.

  • Grayscale Bitcoin Mini Trust ETF (NYSEARCA:BTC): -18.7%
  • Bitwise Bitcoin ETF (NYSEARCA:BITB): -18%
  • iShares Ethereum Trust ETF (ETHA): -30.7%
  • MicroStrategy (MSTR): -26.9%
  • Bit Digital (BTBT): -18.5%
  • Bitdeer Technologies (BTDR): -29%
  • Coinbase (COIN): -24.3%
  • IREN Ltd. (IREN): -16%
  • Mara Holdings (MARA): -21.6%
  • Riot Platforms (RIOT): -19.9%
  • Robinhood (HOOD): +4%

Other bitcoin-backed ETFs are: (IBIT), (ARKB), (GBTC), (BTCO), and (HODL).

Among blockchain ETFs: (BITQ), (DAPP), (BLOK), and (IBLC).

data.title: Eletrobras rises after reaching deal with Brazil over voting power, nuclear plant
data.description:
Eletrobras (NYSE:EBR) +4.3% in Friday's trading after reaching an agreement with Brazil's government in a mediation process that could allow the state to appoint three members of the company's board while freeing the company from fresh investments in the Angra 3 nuclear power plant.

Brazil owns more than 40% of Eletrobras (NYSE:EBR) common shares, but no more than 10% of the stake can be used to exercise voting power over company decisions under the terms of the company's 2022 privatization, which has been criticized by Brazil President Lula da Silva and prompted him to ask the Supreme Court to grant proportional voting power to the government, and the court then pushed the parties into a mediation process.

Under the new agreement, the 10% voting power cap for shareholders will be maintained, but the government will be able to appoint three members of Eletrobras' (EBR) board, which will be expanded to 10 from nine currently.

Eletrobras (EBR) said the two sides would work on a conciliation term to be approved by the company's shareholders and validated by Brazil's Supreme Court as part of the settlement deal.

The company also said it will no longer be obliged to allocate money for Angra 3's completion, but will keep guaranteeing 6.1B reais (~$1.05B) in loans previously obtained for the project during 40 years of off-and-on construction.

Analysts at Itau BBA praised the deal's overall terms as very positive for Eletrobras (EBR) and said having three government-nominated board members is in line with expectations.

data.title: Voya Financial upgraded at Morgan Stanley
data.description:
Morgan Stanley upgraded Voya Financial (NYSE:VOYA) to Overweight from Equalweight on Friday as the company's turnaround plan should drive a rebound in 2025 and set up an attractive growth profile for 2026 and beyond.

Concerns about the longer-term profitability of its Health Solutions segment have been allayed by management's moves to improve performance through new pricing and improved risk selection, said analyst Bob Jian Huang. Stop-loss underwriting normalization could kick in as soon as 2026, he added. Management expects core business growth in Wealth Solutions and Investment Management, supported by steady margins of 35%-39% in Wealth and ~29% in Investment Management.

The provider of workplace benefits and savings products has several paths for improvement, Huang noted. "If executed well, integrations of its recent acquisitions of OneAmerica in Wealth Solutions and Benefitfocus in Health Solutions should yield meaningful earnings benefits over time," he wrote in a note to clients.

Also, investments, such as enhancing capabilities in leave management and disability administration within Health Solutions, and expanding third-party insurance asset management presence, "should yield positive net impacts beyond 2025," Huang said.

Overall, "headwinds, particularly in Health Solutions, should abate, results should stabilize, and both EPS and ROE should inflect higher in the coming years," he said.

Voya (NYSE:VOYA) stock rose 2.4% in Friday morning trading.

Morgan Stanley's Overweight rating on Voya clashes with the SA Quant rating of Sell and aligns with the average Wall Street rating of Buy.

data.title: Perrigo posts record surge on investor day
data.description:
Shares of Perrigo (NYSE:PRGO) jumped ~19% to record potentially the stock's best intraday gain ever as the consumer healthcare firm detailed its near-term and long-term financial targets at its just-concluded Virtual Investor Day.

The two-hour event followed Ireland-based PRGO's Q4 2024 financials on Thursday, which exceeded Street forecasts for earnings but fell short of revenue expectations amid a ~2% YoY drop in net sales due mainly to forex headwinds and discontinued product lines.

Ahead of the investor day on Friday, the company set its 2025 outlook, indicating 1%-3% YoY and 2.5% - 4.5% YoY growth for its all-in and organic net sales, respectively, compared to ~6% YoY and ~5% YoY declines in 2024.

Its outlook for 2025 adj. diluted earnings per share stood at $2.90–$3.10, implying 13%–21% YoY growth compared to flat growth last year.

Additionally, Perrigo (NYSE:PRGO) announced its long-term outlook, indicating 2.5%–4.5% and high-single to low-double-digit percentage CAGR in organic net sales and adjusted diluted EPS, respectively.

data.title: LeMaitre cut to perform by Oppenheimer on valuation
data.description:
Oppenheimer has downgraded LeMaitre Vascular (NASDAQ:LMAT) to perform from outperform as its price target of $93 has been met.

The investment bank added it was downgrading the stock and removing its price target "as we question the longer-term sustainability of consistent price hikes that have led to LMAT's premium valuation."

LeMaitre (NASDAQ:LMAT) released its Q4 earnings report late Thursday, which was in line with expectations, according to Oppenheimer.
